scope: Wrap the existing internal/agent.Run tool loop and the model.NewClient-resolved OAI/Responses clients as ONE hardened in-process driver behind the driver.Driver contract, with the verifier role running the tool loop for investigation before emitting its verdict via exactly one ChatStructured call (closes the sworn#55 gap for in-process dispatch).

reachability.evidence: go test ./internal/driver/inprocess/ -run TestInprocessImplementer -v: InProcess.Dispatch drives the REAL internal/agent.Run loop against an httptest /chat/completions server scripting a tool-call turn then a terminal turn — the bash tool actually executes (echo hi) inside a real `git init` worktree that AssertWorktree (Rule 11) has gated, and the Result carries the loop's final text plus accumulated InputTokens=24/OutputTokens=12, DurationMS>0, provider-confirmed ModelID, CostSource=estimated. TestInprocessVerifier exercises the full verifier path end-to-end through the same integration point: investigation loop (tool executed), then exactly ONE ChatStructured (response_format) call whose request body demonstrably carries the replayed transcript (role:tool message asserted in the raw bytes), verdict returned unmodified in StructuredJSON. TestInprocessContentAlwaysPresent inspects the raw second-request bytes for the present content field (AC-03). Real-provider integration proof is S10's conformance SIT (spec risks R-01); the engine does not call Dispatch until S06 — the Driver contract is the integration point that owns this affordance (ADR-0012).

delivered.item: AC-01: Role=implementer executes the multi-turn tool loop (internal/agent.Run) rooted at WorktreeRoot after the S01 AssertWorktree guard; Status=ok, ResultText=loop final text, InputTokens/OutputTokens accumulated across turns, DurationMS populated

AC-02: Role=verifier runs the tool loop for investigation then obtains the verdict via exactly one ChatStructured call over the accumulated transcript against DispatchInput.VerdictSchema, returned unmodified in Result.StructuredJSON (engine still owns verifier-verdict-v1 validation)
delivered.evidence: internal/driver/inprocess/inprocess_test.go:TestInprocessVerifier (asserts structuredRequestCount==1 and the transcript's role:tool message in the verdict request body)

delivered.item: AC-03 (narrowed to the oai-inprocess chat identity per captain-proceed.md pin 3): a tool-only (empty content) assistant turn still serializes a present content field, asserted on the raw request body bytes the driver sends

AC-04: loop exits on no-tool-calls with the turn cap as circuit breaker; max-turns -> Status=error ErrKind=transient (retryable); structured-emission failure on the verifier path (empty choices / non-object content / empty content) -> ErrKind=protocol; never a panic, never a fabricated verdict (StructuredJSON asserted empty on failure)

AC-05: no edits to internal/agent or internal/model — the diff (files_changed above, live git diff start_commit..HEAD) touches only internal/driver/ files and slice docs; go test ./internal/driver/... passes including S01's untouched TestNoWireImports

Coach ack pin 1 (Type-1 D5): Dispatch returns the error chain carrying the underlying *model.Error so the engine's model.IsTerminal terminal-halt keeps firing pre-S06; auth 401 -> ErrKindAuth, credits 402 -> "credits", both proven terminal
Coach ack pin 2: shared driver.ErrKind* constants used throughout; KindAuth -> driver.ErrKindAuth is an explicit switch arm (errKindFromModel), not a String() collision
Coach ack pin 6: a classified provider error on the verdict call keeps its real ErrKind rather than folding into protocol
Coach ack pins 4+5: status.json.design_decisions populated (D1/D5 Type-1 Coach-decided, D2-D7 + placement Type-2); CostSource="estimated" nominal placeholder for S08

D7 fail-closed guards: empty WorktreeRoot / non-worktree / model-resolution failure / chat-incapable client -> ErrKindConfig; verifier client without StructuredOutput -> ErrKindProtocol before the investigation loop spends tokens
D1 two registered identities off one struct: NewOAIChat()="oai-inprocess", NewOAIResponses()="oai-responses-inprocess", both declaring implementer+verifier and not captain

not_delivered.item: AC-03 test coverage for the oai-responses-inprocess identity
not_delivered.reason: Rule 2 deferral resolved at design review: moot-by-construction — internal/model/openai_responses.go convertMessages emits tool-calling assistant turns as pure function_call input items, so the Responses wire format structurally cannot express the tool-only content-drop; the fix location (internal/model) is out of scope per AC-05.
not_delivered.tracking: captain-proceed.md pin 3 (Coach-acknowledged narrowing; no spec scope change required)

divergence: Driver placement: subpackage internal/driver/inprocess/ (package inprocess) instead of the spec touchpoints' literal internal/driver/inprocess.go — ADR-0012 pins 'internal/driver itself imports neither internal/model nor internal/agent' and S01's TestNoWireImports enforces it over every file in that directory, so the literal touchpoint path could never pass AC-05's own test command. The alternative (exempting files in imports_test.go) would weaken a Coach-ratified Type-1 invariant and edit outside touchpoints. Full rationale in journal.md; recorded as a Type-2 decision in status.json. Planner note: S08's touchpoint reference to internal/driver/inprocess.go should read internal/driver/inprocess/inprocess.go.

sworn llm-check -type ac-satisfaction skipped: exits 'read spec.md: no such file' on this spec-v1 (spec.json) slice — the known format-lag false-negative class (see project memory on release-verify.sh spec.md false-FAIL). Manual AC-to-test cross-check performed instead: every AC has a named test in inprocess_test.go (AC-01 TestInprocessImplementer as named by the AC text itself).

sworn verify (proof-bundle first-pass gate) run with --spec pointed at spec.json returned PASS (captured in reachability.gate_output); the deterministic scripts/release-verify.sh false-FAILs 'spec.md missing' on spec-v1 slices (known, do-not-manufacture-spec.md).
